chartPattern.explanation: The price action shows a rounded bottom forming a cup from approximately mid-January 2026 to early March 2026, with lows around $230-$240. A subsequent handle is forming from early March to early May 2026, with prices consolidating in the $250-$270 range. The current price action on high volume on May 29th, 2026, suggests a potential breakout from this handle.
chartPattern.signal: A confirmed breakout above the handle resistance around $270, especially on increasing volume, could signal a continuation of the prior uptrend, with potential upside targets based on the depth of the cup.

fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue expansion and positive free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a GAAP basis.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 11.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 25.2% YoY, with analyst estimates for the current quarter at 60.1%.
Gross margin of 72.0% indicates strong pricing power and efficiency in its core offering.
Positive Free cash flow (TTM) of $517.61M, suggesting operational cash generation.
Total cash of $2.43B provides a substantial liquidity buffer.
Consensus 'buy' rating from 36 analysts with a mean target price above the current spot.
negatives: Operating margin of -3.6% and Profit margin of -1.1% indicate ongoing losses.
Return on equity and assets are negative, reflecting unprofitability.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.00 suggests significant leverage relative to equity.
Forward P/E of 50.09 and PEG ratio of 1.67 indicate a high valuation.

news.paragraph: Following its recent earnings report, MongoDB has seen a wave of analyst target resets, with most firms maintaining their ratings and raising price targets. Bank of America, Macquarie, UBS, Morgan Stanley, and Wedbush have all adjusted their outlooks, with the mean analyst target now standing at $386.77. While the company reported an earnings beat last quarter, its operating and profit margins remain negative, though free cash flow is positive.

headlines.title: BofA resets MongoDB stock price target after earnings

Morgan Stanley resets MongoDB stock price target after earnings
